title: One field, many faces
testCase: Does every field have the right type?
blocks.0.text: Five fields. All of them text. It works — a person can type anything into any of them.
blocks.1.text: That is exactly the problem. They can type anything.
blocks.2.text: In the quantity field — "lots". In the email — "ivan" and nothing else. In the date — "day after tomorrow". The form accepts it all and sends it with the same confidence.
blocks.4.text: Open this form on a phone. You reach the phone number field and up pops… a letter keyboard. You have to switch to numbers yourself. It is a small thing — and that is exactly why people abandon forms halfway.
blocks.5.text: You told the browser "this is text". It believed you.
blocks.7.items.0: type="email" — checks for an @ and a dot. On a phone it offers a keyboard with @.
blocks.7.items.1: type="tel" — on a phone it offers a number pad.
blocks.7.items.2: type="number" — numbers only, with up-down arrows.
blocks.7.items.3: type="date" — gives a calendar. No more "day after tomorrow".
blocks.7.items.4: type="password" — hides the letters behind dots.
blocks.7.items.5: type="url", type="search", type="color", type="range" — and more.
blocks.9.text: One word of difference. And suddenly the browser knows what it wants from the person — it hands them the right tool and stops them if they get it wrong.
blocks.12.text: Another valueless attribute, like controls on a video: required. Add it — and the form refuses to go while the field is empty.
blocks.14.text: And two more that make a form pleasant: placeholder (the grey text showing an example) and autocomplete (the browser offers what the person has typed elsewhere).
blocks.16.text: Careful: a placeholder is NOT a label. It vanishes the moment the person starts typing. If it is the only thing saying what is wanted, they are left with nothing. Field names have their own tag — the next lesson.
blocks.17.text: And still — do not trust it
blocks.18.text: These checks are for CONVENIENCE, not security. Anyone can switch them off from the browser tools in ten seconds. The real check happens on the server, always.
blocks.19.text: Your turn. Give every field the right type. And make the email required.
